scope: Cross-platform sandbox engine policy model, manager selection, backend argv generation, and macOS policy templates.
_sourceRootBrandingAllow: branding-scan: allow upstream source root path

requiredBehaviors: filesystem and network sandbox policies are represented as structured data rather than legacy string-only modes
special paths, glob denies, read/write roots, protected metadata names, and platform-default flags can be resolved against a sandbox policy cwd
platform sandbox selection distinguishes macOS seatbelt, Linux seccomp, Windows restricted token, and no-sandbox modes
restricted read-only policies never gain implicit cwd write access
edgeCases: relative permission paths resolve against the sandbox policy cwd
glob entries are treated as deny-only constraints for read access

requiredBehaviors: auto, require, and forbid preferences select an initial sandbox from platform and policy requirements
additional command permissions are merged into effective filesystem and network policy before backend argv generation
Linux sandbox handoff preserves command cwd, environment, selected sandbox metadata, and arg0 override
compatibility policy projection preserves full-access, read-only, workspace-write, and external-sandbox legacy modes when representable
edgeCases: auto mode skips sandboxing for unrestricted filesystem plus enabled network
forbid mode returns no sandbox even when policies would otherwise require one
WSL1 bubblewrap incompatibility is surfaced before attempting Linux argv execution

requiredBehaviors: additional permission profiles normalize duplicate grants, reject non-deny globs, and resolve path grants against cwd
merge combines filesystem entries and network grants while preserving constraining glob scan depth
intersection grants only accepted readable paths and retains deny constraints that apply to accepted grants
specific read/deny carveouts and protected metadata paths override broader writable roots
filesystem-root read/write grants are recognized as full-disk access only when not narrowed
read-deny glob entries reject matching granted paths during intersection
edgeCases: empty network/file permission objects collapse away
external filesystem sandbox policy does not force a platform sandbox for disabled network
enabled additional network grants widen restricted network policy to enabled

requiredBehaviors: seatbelt argv contains the generated policy payload, path definition parameters, separator, and original command
file-read and file-write policies are built from resolved readable/writable roots with protected metadata exclusions
proxy-managed network policy narrows outbound access to loopback proxy ports and allowed unix sockets
unreadable filesystem globs become anchored deny regexes for reads and unlink-style writes
edgeCases: proxy values without a scheme are parsed as HTTP URLs
patterns without glob metacharacters deny the exact path and descendants

requiredBehaviors: Linux launcher argv serializes policy cwd, command cwd, permission profile JSON, optional legacy landlock flag, optional proxy-network flag, separator, and command
Linux launcher argv can be handed to a real helper subprocess through the engine test harness
system bubblewrap discovery ignores candidates underneath the current working tree
WSL1 and user namespace failure diagnostics are identified without executing the launcher
edgeCases: missing PATH returns no system bubblewrap candidate
WSL2 microsoft-standard kernel strings are not treated as WSL1
known user namespace stderr fragments map to a user namespace warning
